When the blaster bolts start flying, he often sits back at first, finding the weak point in the enemy’s strategy. Once the enemy notices him, he charges and attacks like a man expecting to die. His allies struggle to see the genius in his tactics, and call him *mir’shupla*, or “brain damaged,” despite how often he rescues them.
gear: Mandalorian heavy battle armour (+2 Soak, +1 Defence)

name: Ak-Mal, Weequay Hunter
description:
Ak-Mal is a ruthless mercenary recently put on retainer by Teemo the Hutt, of the Desilijic kajidic on Tatooine. Ak-Mal has tracked Kad Solus to Vlemoth Port on behalf of Teemo, and has orders to capture or kill Kad and repossess his ship. Hailing from Sriluur, Ak-Mal was born into bondage and was enslaved as muscle for the Desilijic kajidic. Impressed with his skills, Teemo proposed a scheme that earned him his freedom and a generous loan, in exchange for a period of exclusivity working for Teemo.

Ak-Mal is aggressive, famous for charging his enemies head on and finishing them off with his vibro-ax. He often works With a horde of Klatooinian bruisers, who are usually there only to soak up a few blaster bots and confirm stories of his prowess in cantinas. To protect himself, he usually employs two weequay Snipers to cover his reckless advances in combat. He is loyal to Teemo, and convincing him to stop and listen to an offer or hep defend Xorrn is nearly impossible.

name: Weequay Sniper
description: Once he had won his autonomy, Ak-Mal immediately travelled to Sriluur to rescue two of his nieces before they could be enslaved. He hopes they can grow into equal partners one day, but for now they are instructed to hang back and protect Ak-Mal from a distance. Should Ak-Mal suffer too many injuries, one of the sisters attempts to rescue him while the other continues to provide protective fire from behind.

name: Klatooinian Bruiser
description: Ak-Mal travels with a dozen Klatooinian bruisers, who operate in three fire teams of four. They tend to charge aggressively and take cover once they are in range. Most of the henchmen are enslaved to Teemo the Hutt, who allows Ak-Mal to use them as he sees fit.

name: Talz Chieftain
description: The Talz follow the strongest among them. The best chieftains consult with the eldest members of the tribe, while reminding the younger hunters of their strength and prowess to stave off challenges to their rule. Chieftains are responsible for protecting both the tribe and their hunting grounds from outsiders.

name: Talz Hunter
description: The best hunters in any Talz tribe are like ghosts on the tundra, ambushing their prey from within snowdrifts, or slaying a creature with a spear from thirty metres away. The tribe’s very best hunter often aspires to the position of chieftain, a right earned through single combat.

name: Talz Warrior
description: The Talz living in Vlemoth Port’s northern tundra are generally peaceful but very xenophobic. Thanks to frequent clashes with slavers, they view intrusions by outsiders as acts of aggression. Talz warriors tend to own at least one hunting spear which they can either throw or use as a melee weapon. Their bone knives, designed to skin animals, are also useful in combat.

name: Mandalorian Warrior
description: Every sentient being born in Arumorut begins training in combat at a very young age. While many choose one specific skill to specialise in, all Mandalorians at Arumorut pride themselves on their combat prowess. It is common for younger warriors to constantly seek new challenges and ways to test their skills.
gear: Mandalorian heavy battle armour (+2 Soak, +1 Defence)

name: Chieftain Vera Beroya
description:
Vera became clan leader two years ago, when her father was killed (or so some think) defending the Talz from Zygerrian slavers. At twenty-eight years of age, Vera has led Mandalorians into combat countless times and has earned their respect for her tactical mind and combat prowess. Decisive and confident on the battlefield, Vera is less sure of herself in her new role as civic leader. She agonises over decisions that affect her entire clan. She relishes every chance she has to go to battle, and still leads her clan from the front.

An imposing figure, Vera is a tall, muscular woman with the powerful build of a human who has dedicated her entire life to warfare. She carries a light repeating blaster into combat, and when her ammo runs out—and it always does—she drops her weapon and charges to the very front lines. Screaming the entire time, she swings a massive mythosaur axe that belonged to her father, destroying everything in her path like a Mandalorian berserker of legend.
notes: The SHUKOR-05 Light repeater has either Autofire or Slow Firing 2
gear: Heavy Beskar’gam armour (+2 Soak, +2 Defence, Cortosis)

name: Zygerrian Slaver
description:
Nearly every inhabitant of the Outer Rim knows of, and rightfully fears, the slavers of Zygerria. Vicious and bold, Zygerrians will stop at nothing to subjugate their prey, as their very way of life revolves around the slave trade. This dedication makes Zygerrians the most prolific slavers in the galaxy.

In combat, Zygerrian slavers usually resort to non-lethal tactics in order to maximise the number of prisoners captured. However, they are not above making examples out of rowdy prisoners in order to terrify and intimidate other slaves.

name: Zygerrian Royal Guard
description: The Zygernanc; who serve within the royal guard are the elite soldiers or the Zygerrian Slave Empire. Their duties are to protect the royal family and their holdings. and to help ensure that slavery within the Empire thrives. These brutes patrol the streets of Sclavos, enforcing the Molec family’s rule and defending it from equally malicious rival slavers. Zygerrian royal guards are trained to corner, subdue, and capture their adversaries so they may be sod into slavery, but they do not hesitate to resort to lethal methods if necessary.

name: Nam Beroya
description:
Not all Mandalorians shared the pacifistic views expressed by Duchess Satine Kryze during her rise to power on Mandalore. Nam Beroya was the Clan Awaud chieftain on Mandalore at that time. He saw no future in Satine’s great vision or of the Death Watch’s barbaric reversion to galactic conquest as part of the Shadow Collective. Beroya favoured the old ways, the path of the honourable nomadic mercenary. While Beroya’s clan fractured along factional lines, he followed his own vision and led more than three hundred like-minded Mandalorians to the snowy, evergreen forests of Vlemoth Port’s northern latitudes to live by the old ways. Over time, Beroya’s clan swelled to over eight hundred combat-ready warriors, and with the assistance of his adopted daughter, Vera, Nam Beroya led his clan on countless missions.

Two years ago, while Nam was on a hunting expedition with a local tribe of Talz, a band of Zygerrian slavers attacked, and Nam was captured defending his allies. Beroya’s clan thought he had perished in the battle, dooming him to a life of enslavement without the possibility of rescue. Prince Molec threw Beroya into his gladiatorial arena to fight for his court’s amusement, and the Mandalorian has run an undefeated streak for two years.

Nam Beroya is a burly, middle-aged man of Concordian descent, with olive skin covered in scars from countless battles. He possesses a cunning tactical mind and projects an air of confidence and competence that commands respect and inspires those serving under him. When leading his clan, Beroya carefully weighs every decision with care for how it would affect his people, but once he arrives at a conclusion. he charges forward like an angry reek.
notes: Temporarily lame. Until this :hard: Critical Injury is healed, Nam cannot perform more than one maneuver during his turn.

name: Jorec and Mard Gemen
description:
Jorec and Mard faithfully served King Molec, and Queen Scintel before him, as the royal archivists, but they were reassigned as registrars on Kowak to oversee the prince’s actions on behalf of the crown. The two have served as a roadblock to the prince’s youthful decadence ever since. Come auction night, the ageing brothers take every precaution to ensure a successful and profitable evening. The two are convinced the prince’s entire operation would fall down around his ears were it not for their guidance.

No-one would suspect Jorec and Mard are fraternal twins. Jorec is a large, broadly built Zygerrian who is friendly, but simple and unkempt. His brother Mard is short and wiry, and overcompensates for his less-than-intimidating stature with an abrasive attitude and meticulous grooming.

While proud to serve the crown, Jorec and Mard resent being on a backwater to babysit the prince, whom they consider an embarrassment to the royal family. Their anger at the king has forced them to question their entire way of life. The twins’ loyalty to the crown is weakened by their burning desire to return home to Zygerria, preferably at the expense of the prince. The latter impulse can be exploited to convince them to help the Rebellion.
notes: Jorec has the Kill with Kindness talent while Mard has the Scathing Tirade talent.
gear: Holo-messenger (with direct line to King Molec

name: Captain Rajim Armin
description:
A long-time servant of the Molec royal house, Captain Rajim Armin advised Prime Minister Atai Molec during the Clone Wars, when Molec served under Queen Scintel. When the Zygerrian Slavers Guild allied with the Confederacy of Independent Systems, Captain Armin served on Prime Minister Molec’s war council. Rajim’s uncanny ability to read others and his skill as a duellist also made him the ideal bodyguard for the prime minister whom he continued to serve when the prime minister became King Atai Molec.

Captain Armin served as King Molec’s eyes and ears during the king’s sons’ fateful tour of Zygerrian holdings. When the elder heir was killed in a skirmish with Imperials, Armin shared the blame and was relieved of his duties to the throne. Armin was relegated to serve as the younger prince’s second-in-command on Kowak, far away from the capital.

Captain Armin is a tall, angular Zygerrian noble of great stature and impeccable breeding. He never strays too far from the prince, standing at attention in perfect military dress. Armin is incredibly smart, terribly insightful, and painfully bored with his station at the prince’s side. Despite the monotony of his role and the shame of his demotion Captain Armin takes great pride in his work and always does what is best for the throne.

name: Prince Sono Molec
description:
Sono Molec inherited control of Kowak from his father, King Atai Molec. The Prince oversees slaving operations in the southern Slice, supplying the bulk of Twi’lek, Rodian, Theelin, and Togruta slaves to both the Hutt Empire and the larger auction house on Zygerria. Sono also runs a poaching operation, supplying Kowakian monkey-lizards to buyers all over the galaxy. Since assuming control of Kowak, Prince Molec has divided slave supply worlds in the Trailing Sectors with both the Thalassian slavers and the Karazak Slavers’ Cooperative.

Despite his “accomplishments” Prince Molec has never earned the respect of his father. Two years ago, Sono was touring Zygerrian holdings in the southern Slice with his older brother Hordem and recklessly got drawn into a naval engagement with Captain Nervi. The batlle ended with the defeat of the Molec forces and the death of Sono’s brother. The death of the king’s first heir drove a wedge between Sono and his father that ended in his pseudo-exile to Kowak.

Prince Molec maintains a sabacc face as unreadable as ferrocrete, making him hard to read during negotiations. He leads a decadent lifestyle both personally and professionally, and he has many vices, including alcohol, gambling, and spice. Prince Molec also takes a morbid pleasure in the misery of others. However, beneath his egotistical facade, the prince is paranoid. Sono is convinced that servants within his court actively plot to depose him, which slowly chips away at his self-assured exterior.

name: Trandoshan Slaver
description: Resolute and ferocious, Trandoshan slavers are among the vilest groups in the entire underworld. While they specialise in the slavery of WooKiees, they rarely refuse the opportunity to put their combat prowess to the test. No matter whether the prey is a creature or a sentient being, a Trandoshan lives for the thrill of the hunt.

name: Baron Dorn Sevelle
description: A human trade baron from Raithal, Dorn Sevelle is a representative of the KSC, and he was until recently a close friend of Prince Molec. The two often sat together during slave gladiatorial matches, wagering on the outcomes. The latest such bet resulted in Sevelle’s walking away with the prince’s top prizefighter. Realising the warrior is more trouble than he is worth, Baron Sevelle sold the gladiator to the Thalassians. Now he awaits their arrival to pick up their prize.

name: Karazak Acquisition Specialist
description: Careful planning goes into every potential Karazak Slavers’ Cooperative operation. Acquisition specialists scout planets and hyperspace routes all over the Outer Rim, looking for new markets and “product species.”

name: Karazak Acquisition Agent
description: Acquisitions agents conduct all “product acquisition” (enslaving) operations for the KSC with military efficiency. Acquisitions agents commonly frequent major hyperlanes in the Outer Rim, but each successful operation emboldens them to operate further Coreward.

name: Thalassian Slaver
description: Members of a society of slavers thousands of years old, Thalassians are some of the most resourceful slave traders in the galaxy. Even though their shell-covered bodies would normally inhibit their mobility, the cunning reptiles rely on various technologies, such as repulsors and jetpacks, to travel quickly. Thalassians are rarely without their armour or array of gadgets. Seeing most other species as nothing more than animals, they utilise electronets, repulsor traps, stun grenades, and all manner of non-lethal weapons to round up and ensnare their targets. These slavers operate out of the New Territories, using ion cannon– equipped Z-95 Headhunters to prey on unsuspecting hyperspace travellers, but they are also common elsewhere in the galaxy.
gear: Repulsor equipped heavy battle armour (+2 Soak, +1 Defence)

name: Gasch N’doss
description: Gasch N’doss is a gaunt Rutian Twi’lek with faded, ashen-blue skin. He has been a slave since he was a teenage boy, so he can barely remember what it means to be free. N’doss has been abused both verbally and physically his entire adult life, desensitising him to threats. He has fielded lie after lie from his Zygerrian enslavers, making him cynical and sceptical. The aged Twi’lek is, however, a plain and practical character who is unapologetic about getting what is best for his fellow slaves.

name: Captain Ralchio Nervi
description:
Born Ralchio Sargan out of an affair between Vilola Sargan and Jans Coorsa, Ralchio grew up knowing his true parentage, but forbidden from discussing it. Still, he felt entitled to both Jans Coorsa’s position as cacique and to the strange, almost mystical techniques Jans had used to combat the Republic and later the Empire on Gall. Ralchio fought alongside Jans, but he always felt his birth father treated him worse than the Imperials ever did. Eventually, he was driven to extremes by the emotional distress, and he confronted his father, demanding to know his secrets. When Jans refused, Ralchio gave in to rage and blacked out. When he woke, Jans was dead, despite the lack of any sign of external damage.

Ralchio fled. He decided to join the Empire, convinced his family would never think to look for him there. Before joining, he married Lila Nervi, a member of the influential Nervi family. Ralchio cautiously explored his connection to the Force, but went to extremes to hide his powers for fear of Imperial discovery. His paranoia convinced him an Imperial assault on Xorrn could expose him not just as an associate of traitors to the Empire, but as a Force user. Eventually, he knew the only way to protect his secrets would be to launch an attack on Xorrn himself and destroy any evidence.

abilities.name: Unnatural Luck
abilities.description: Twice per session, can roll :force::force: after an NPC or PC makes a check for an activity that occurs within line of sight, but before the check is resolved; may spend :forcepip: to remove :advantage: or add :threat:, or :forcepip::forcepip: to remove :success: or add :failure:.

name: Agent Nath Alico
description:
Operation Ruby Audit was a Rebel Alliance deep-cover mission in which an undercover agent operated for months as Imperial Navy Lieutenant Nath Alico. The Nath Alico cover identity has been serving as Captain Nervi’s adjutant, with access to Imperial Navy classified data as well as fleet movements. It is only because of agent Alico’s quick thinking that Foundry Four has an opportunity to reclaim the secrecy of its location.

The agent has assumed several different cover identities during her time with Alliance Intelligence, though often low-level Imperial officers and functionaries. She is meticulous and prepares for almost every contingency far in advance.

name: Lt. Colonel Hovlinn
description: When the battle moves to Xorrn’s surface, Captain Nervi leaves the details of approach to the management of Erle Hovlinn

name: Nel Henol
description: An enthusiastic, practiced bargainer and negotiator, Nel Henol is always pushing a new service or sale to her customers. She’s also on the Gorensla payroll, acting as the kajidic’s eyes and ears for any unusual activity at Bog Bay.

name: Riar Royal Guard
description: Local Royal Guard Patrol Officers monitor Bog Bay for criminal activities. Though not as elite as the ones in Toydor, they can be formidable against the local criminal element. One of their most important duties is to ensure that the flight restrictions in the area are followed; they are notoriously uncompromising concerning any violations they find.

name: Toydarian Infochant
description: The PCs might wish to confer with a local infochant to gain information concerning Walisi or the statue. Several are in the Bog Bay area. Developing one or more as well-paid contacts could not only gain the PCs a valuable resource but also help establish a false pretence if they are using aliases as part of their hunt.

name: RT-3PO
description: Brought on to aid in translations and negotiations, RT-3PO has excelled especially in running Walisi’s auctions. It is loyal and competent but scarcely a fighter, and it was lucky not to have been seriously damaged in the attack.
notes: Suffering 4 Wounds, malfunctioning visual system (photoreceptors).

name: Cleaning Droid
description: Walisi purchased a series of these droids some time ago. He has since upgraded them regularly to ensure they properly maintain his establishment without damaging and of his items through overzealous dusting or improperly applied cleaning liquids.

weapons.name: Built-in cleaning spray hose
weapons.notes:
Disorient can be activated if sprayed in the target’s eyes.

Knockdown can be activated if sprayed on the floor, in which case the cleaning fluid remains on the floor for the rest of the encounter causing all characters in the area to make a :hard: Coordination check or fall prone.

name: Wrecker
description: One of the more prominent mercenaries operating in the region, Wrecker styles himself as more than just a cheap tough. Lately, he has been branching out into information brokering. Although the bulk of his credits still come from violence — like the theft and kidnapping the PCs are investigating — he knows time catches up with everyone, and that he needs to shift to a less physical line of work at some point if he wants to maintain his power base.

name: Toydarian Agent
description:
Jerserra has a small group of agents of Toydaria. Its members keep a low profile due to the Hutt operations here. Led by “Boss” Trelon, the group was an almost-forgotten part of her growing power base until one of its members reported news of the auction. Immediately recognising the shard within the satue, Jerserra ordered the agents to arrange to retrieve it and kidnap its seller. Now, they hold Walisi and *Tragic Hope*, awaiting her arrival.

The GM can use the following profile as needed for Loorek, Ileon, or any of the other local Toydarians who server Jerserra on this world.

name: Walisi
description: Walisi has run his shop for years now, slowly rising in influence across Toydaria and becoming known at an interstellar level. Though he has been somewhat concerned about how much power the Gorensla kajidic has over him, so far the arrangement has been mutually profitable. He had anticipated that the auction of *Tragic Hope* would be his grandest achievement yet, but he was quite unprepared for the unwanted attention it brought — perhaps enough to make the statue his final acquisition.
notes: Suffering 7 Wounds, 6 Strain, and 7 Critical Injuries.

name: Preelos Consortium Customised Security Droid
description: These droids are deployed throughout the mansion and normally they are quite effective in maintaining security. When faced with the superior firepower that the Gorensla enforcers bring to bear at the start of their brash attack, however, they are little more than a distraction. On the other hand, later on, as the Gorensla forces are thinned, these droids could help turn the tide.

name: ISB Security Droid
description: Jerserra’s agents earlier stole and reprogrammed these more advanced and dangerous ISB model to patrol the mansion as well. They more than make up for their lower numbers with superior firepower and armour plating.

name: Gorensla Weequay Enforcer
description: The Gorensla kajidic has many blasters-for-hire on its payroll. Most don’t survive their first assignment, but there are always more to be found. The clan uses Weequay enforcers quite often, some of whom have already been operating as bounty hunters or bodyguards.

name: Pravon, Gorensla Lieutenant
description: Pravon has led one of the Gorensla’ primary enforcer groups for only a few months, and he is eager to prove himself worthy of even higher advancement. He has extensive cybernetics, including an artificial voice box. This allows Pravon to be even louder than he was before, something he uses effectively to goad his fellow Weequay into even greater acts of violence against the kajidic’s enemies.

name: “Boss” Trelon
description: Trelon has gone by “Boss” since Jerserra assigned him as commander of her smuggling/information-gathering group on Toydaria, and he has lived up to the nickname. He’s developed a wide net of informants through intimidation and blackmail in order to further her cause (and his own). While loyal to Jerserra, he’s also ambitious. He hopes to impress her enough with his work that she advances him to greater power within her operation.

name: Jerserra’s Mercenary
description: Over the years, Jerserra has gathered many unsavoury types from a variety of Outer Rim species to her side. Most are in it simply for the credits, but some feel she is more than just another merc leader. She has vision, and aiding her can only lead to powerful roles for themselves in the future she is building.
notes: Armed with either a blaster carbine or a vibro-ax.

name: Jerserra (Early)
description: It’s been many years since she left Dathomir, but Jerserra never forgot the legends of the Nardithi. Through her recent Force visions from the shard she possesses, she feels sure that finally she has found a link that will reveal the resting place of the lost cluster.

abilities.name: Force Power: Jerserra’s Influence
abilities.description: Jerserra may spend :forcepip: and target one character within Short range. That character must immediately make an :average: fear check as an out-of-turn Incidental. If the target suffers Strain as a result of that fear check, she recovers from an equal amount of Strain. She may also spend :forcepip: to extend the power’s range to Medium.

abilities.name: Force Power: Move
abilities.description: Jerserra may spend :forcepip: to move one Silhouette 0 object within Short range to another location within Short range. She can spend :forcepip: to increase range one band, spend :forcepip: to increase Silhouette movable to 1, and spend :forcepip: to increase number of objects movable at one time by one (to a maximum of two). Jerserra can hurl objects by making a Move Force power check and rolling a ranged attack as part of the pool. The attack’s difficulty is equal to the Silhouette of the object being thrown and only succeeds if she can spend enough :forcepip: to move the object. The attack deals damage equal to the Silhouette of the object times 10 (Silhouette 0 deals 5 damage) plus one per :success:. To throw multiple objects, she must generate enough :forcepip: to move multiple objects, and must use rules for Auto-Fire to determine difficulty and targeting.

abilities.name: Force Power: Unleash
abilities.description: Jerserra makes a Force power check and targets one enemy at Short range, and rolls a ranged attack as part of the pool, using an :average: Discipline check instead of normal difficulty. If the check is successful and generates :forcepip::forcepip: the attack deals 3 damage with a critical rating of 4, plus 1 additional damage per :success:. May spend :forcepip: to increase damage by 2, and :forcepip: to increase range to Medium.

weapons.name: Unleash
weapons.notes: May spend :forcepip: to increase damage by 2 and :forcepip: to increase range to Medium.

name: Irrica the Hutt
description: Irrica’s life on Toydaria is near perfect. As Gorensla’s leader in Riar, she enjoys a great deal of power, albeit on the local stage. The ongoing rivalry with the Desilijic kajidic, however, has reached an intolerable level; she has started to see Desilijic’s machinations (whether real or imagined) in each recent setback and failure.
gear: Other equipment available from kajidic upon demand

name: Grax and Gremka
description: Twin Toydarians from a village outside of Arnel, Grax and Gremka work mostly as annra farmers, but they ave made a side operation out of leading expeditions into the surrounding swamplands. Their rapid-fire negotiating style, whereby they seem to argue more with each other than actually haggle over their fees as guides, is often very effective with those unprepared for their act.

abilities.name: Twin Negotiators
abilities.description: When working together, can spend :advantage::advantage: to add automatic :success: or :threat::threat: to add automatic :failure: when making opposed social checks.

name: Siluuk Eel
description:
A valued source of food, these snakelike fish range from one to eight metres in length and have glowing rings running the length of their bodies. Farmers in Arnel grow and harvest them in huge numbers for local sale and shipment off-world. Tending is an athletic endeavour, as the eels fight the farmers each step of the process, often by leaping several metres out of the water to snap at one of the hovering Toydarians.

Siluuk Eels can be found in a variety of sizes depending on their maturity, but all can be aggressive regardless of their size.

abilities.name: Siluuk Glow
abilities.description: In darkness, emits a phosphorescent glow that can be as illuminating as a bright light.

name: Yeel Dresk
description: Yeel has seen his triumphant sale of *Tragic Hope* turn from riches in credits to a ruin of a crashed starship. As his lack of repair skills is matched only by his stubbornness, he has yet to leave the planet. Now stranded in a roaring river as flood waters rise, Yeel is beginning to feel that perhaps he might finally be amenable to outside help.
gear: Other equipment aboard the Tireless Wanderer

name: ISB Agent Lorn Bal
description: Bal has never enjoyed his assignment on Toydaria, as Hutt domination there means he cannot wield Imperial power as fully as he would desire. Faced with the opportunity to pursue someone he believes to be associated with the mysterious mercenary under ISB scrutiny, Bal is eager to deploy his forces far from Hutt attention and to grab Dresk and anyone with him for interrogation.

name: Stormtrooper: Jet Trooper
description: A constant sight across the galaxy, stormtroopers exist as a constant reminder of the Empire’s power. On Toydaria, some squads deploy wearing jetpacks, allowing them to counter any targets who seek to escape into the skies.
notes: At the Game Master’s discretion one member of each minion group may be armed with a light repeating blaster with sling.
gear: Jetpack (Allows user to function as a Silhouette 1, Speed 2, Handling 0, System Strain Threshold 3 vehicle that can only operate in atmosphere and requires Piloting: Planetary to operate)

name: Riar Air Patrol Pursuit Drone
description: These disc-shaped drones are fast, but not particularly well armoured. They are designed to capture suspects rather than destroy their craft, and so are armed with potent ion mesh arrays. As they are relatively cheap but effective, Riar Starport authorities use them in high number to ensure that air traffic routes remain clear.
notes: The provided weapon profile is vehicle scale.

abilities.name: Latch-On
abilities.description: Once at Close range, make an opposed Piloting: Planetary check against target vehicle to affix drone to target.

abilities.name: Jet Powered
abilities.description: While aloft, the drone functions as a Silhouette 1, Speed 3, Handling +2, System Strain Threshold 2 vehicle.

name: Red-Eye Dren
description: Dren has operated deep within Kwenn for many years now, trading titbits of information for credits and more information. His signature glowing eyes are only the most obvious of his cybernetic replacements. Through his access to a wide variety of databases, Dren has made himself invaluable to criminal elements throughout the region.

abilities.name: Customised Cybernetic Eyes
abilities.description: Add :advantage::advantage: to Perception checks; add :setback: to Negotiation and similar checks made against Dren by beings looking at his eyes.

weapons.name: Optical flare
weapons.notes:
Characters who see the flare must make a :daunting: Resilience check or become blind for two rounds per :failure: and gain a :setback: for every :threat: until the end of the encounter.

A failed check with :despair: means the target is Blinded as per the Critical Injury Result table in the Force and Destiny Core Rulebook, page 225.

name: Sprantal
description: Sprantal are massive amphibians with thick hides, long legs, and longer necks. They are typically a deep mottled-green colour that helps camouflage them, particularly underwater. Their flat heads have three spherical eyes on top in a triangular arrangement, giving them a 360-degree view. When they hide underwater, their heads are just under the surface, allowing the dark-coloured eyes to remain above the water to watch for potential prey and enemies. Sprantal have an additional, forward-facing eye below their wide, toothy mouth that allows them to see underwater as well as giving them a better view when attacking prey. Their stout bodies are approximately three to four metres long and half again as wide. Their necks can extend in a highly flexible manner, allowing sprantals when standing at full height to attack creatures everywhere except directly to the rear.

abilities.name: Sprantal Spit
abilities.description: If Blast activates, any additional targets that are hit suffer the same effects as the target. Ensnare requires :advantage: to activate. Any creature ensnared may attempt an Athletics check to free itself: the difficulty starts at :average: and increases by one level each round, to a maximum of :formidable:. After three hours the spit becomes brittle and falls apart.

name: Belonuk (Adult)
description: Belonuks are powerful quadrupeds. They have a bone-like armoured head, which they use to ram enemies and knock them over. Their powerful necks do not allow for much range of motion, but their deep-set eyes are widely spaced, giving them an excellent field of vision. Adult belonuks have sharp claws and even sharper needle-like teeth that can pierce the hide of even the toughest of Dathomir’s legendary creatures.

abilities.name: Belonuk Charge
abilities.description: Make an opposed Brawl check vs. Athletics or Coordination: if the Belonuk is successful the target is knocked prone, suffering Wounds equal to :success: and Strain equal to :advantage:.

abilities.name: Belonuk Trample
abilities.description: Against an opponent that is already prone or is one Silhouette size smaller than the belonuk, then as an action, make an opposed Brawl vs. Coordination or Resilience check. If the belonuk is successful, target is knocked prone until the end of their next turn and suffers Wounds equal to :success: and Strain equal to :advantage:.

name: Belonuk (Young)
description: The younger of the species refrain from charging individually, instead using swarm tactics to overwhelm their foes. They minimise their exposure to other predators by grabbing their prey and hiding them away as quickly as possible.

abilities.name: Belonuk Pack Tactics
abilities.description: Add :boost: to the combat check for each belonuk in the group.

abilities.name: Belonuk Scramble
abilities.description: May grab a prone target with a successful Brawl check and immediately drag the target one range band; the target is immobilised by the pack, and subsequent attempts to drag the target require an opposed Athletics check.

name: Vursonk
description: The vursonk is a small scavenging creature with six strong, whip-like tentacles equally spaced around the perimeter of an egg-shaped body, Its mouth is on the underside, with flaps that allow it to expand its maw and grab objects almost as large as itself. The creature has multiple eyes on each side of its body, giving it excellent all-around vision in light, and infrared vision in the darkness. A vursonk latches onto organic matter and either eats it in place or launches itself into the air for a fast getaway, taking the food back to a safe area for storage until later. It may make several trips if there is a ready supply of potential sustenance.

abilities.name: Force Power: Enhance
abilities.description: When making an Athletics check, the vursonk combines it with an Enhance power check, rolling :force:; may spend :darkside: to gain :success: or :advantage:, or may spend :lightside: by using a Destiny Point and suffering 1 Wound per :lightside: used.

abilities.name: Infrared Vision
abilities.description: The vursonk can see heat signatures up to thirty metres away.

weapons.name: Teeth
weapons.notes: When the vursonk leaps onto a targeted object, it envelops the object in its mouth and attempts to jump away with it.

name: Eerig, Nightbrother Lieutenant
description: One of several Nightbrother seconds under Freff’s leadership, Eerig had been uneasy about this mission. This feeling has diminished, though, as the Nightbrothers have followed Jerserra deeper into the ruins of the lost ship. He and his group are now ready to fight for her, no matter the threats they face from her foes or the dangerous surroundings.

name: Nightbrother
description: Like all Nightbrothers, these Zabrak males seem to exist only to serve the Nightsisters. When Jerserra returned to Dathomir, this local tribe quickly came to her side to fight off any who would oppose her.

name: Freff, Nightbrother Leader
description: Freff is highly honoured to be part of Jerserra’s quest, having seen first-hand not only her powers with the Force but also her resources as a mercenary leader. Now that she is deep underground with the Nardithi cluster in her command, his awe of her has only grown, while her powers have increased.
gear: Datapad with pathway guidance notations and mapping protocols enabled

abilities.name: Force Power: Unleash
abilities.description: The manifested Narditihi Nightsister makes a Force power check targeting one enemy at Short range, and rolls a ranged attack as part of the pool, using an :average: Discipline check instead of normal difficulty. If the check is successful and generates :forcepip::forcepip:, the attack deals 3 damage with a critical rating of 4, plus 1 additional per :success:. May spend :forcepip: to increase damage by 2 and :forcepip: to increase range to Medium.

weapons.name: Unleash
weapons.notes: May spend :forcepip: to increase damage by 2 and :forcepip: to increase range to Medium.

name: Jerserra (Late)

abilities.name: Force Power: Jerserra’s Influence
abilities.description: Jerserra may spend :forcepip: and target one character per :forcepip: within Short range. That character must immediately make a :hard: fear check as an out-of-turn Incidental. If the target suffers Strain as a result of that fear check, she recovers an amount of Strain equal to the total suffered. She may also spend :forcepip: to target one additional character, and :forcepip: to extend the power’s range to Medium.

abilities.name: Force Power: Move
abilities.description: Jerserra may spend :forcepip: to move one Silhouette 0 object within Short range to another location within Short range. She can spend :forcepip: to increase range one band, spend :forcepip: to increase Silhouette movable to 1, and spend :forcepip: to increase number of objects movable at one time by one (to a maximum of two). Jerserra can hurl objects by making a Move Force power check and rolling a ranged attack as part of the pool. The attack’s difficulty is equal to the Silhouette of the object being thrown and only succeeds if she can spend enough :forcepip: to move the object. The attack deals damage equal to the Silhouette of the object times 10 (Silhouette 0 deals 5 damage) plus one per :success:. To throw multiple objects, she must generate enough :forcepip: to move multiple objects, and must use rules for Auto-Fire to determine difficulty and targeting.

abilities.name: Force Power: Unleash
abilities.description: Jerserra makes a Force power check and targets one enemy at Short range, and rolls a ranged attack as part of the pool, using an :average: Discipline check instead of normal difficulty. If the check is successful and generates :forcepip::forcepip: the attack deals 3 damage with a critical rating of 4, plus 1 additional damage per :success:. May spend :forcepip: to increase damage by 2, and :forcepip: to increase range to Medium.

weapons.name: Unleash
weapons.notes: May spend :forcepip: to increase damage by 2 and :forcepip: to increase range to Medium.

gear: Hand-held holoprojector with pathway guidance notations and mapping protocols enabled

name: Stormtrooper: Cavetrooper
description: Certain detachments of the Imperial stormtrooper corps are trained for rapid response actions in a variety of environments, allowing them to quickly move through a particular kind of terrain to reach and destroy a given target. The troops stationed on Phemis are from such a training programme, and have traded in their standard issue armaments for carbines and long-bladed vibroknives more appropriate to combat in the planet’s cavern systems. They also wear lighter, flexible armour similar to that worn by scout troopers.
gear: Low-light vision gear (remove :setback::setback: from checks due to darkness)

name: Pehmis Canyon Charger
description: These huge quadrupeds measure nearly four metres tall at the shoulder, and their blocky construction makes them more than six metres in length from the tips of their stub-like tails to the grinding beaks with which they tear apart their prey. Their most notable feature is the massive crest emerging from their skull—a thick ridge of bone that curves forward past the flattened face of the beast and is used as its primary weapon.

name: Rubat Spinner
description: The ten-legged rubat spinner is far sturdier than its spindly limbs would indicate. Although its bulbous central mass is under half a metre in length, the dense minerals strengthening its carapace allow it to out-mass and overpower prey more than twice its size.

name: “Ironarm”, Stormtrooper Commander
description:
Although the Stormtrooper Corps largely discourages individuality among its members, some unique warriors arise who develop a name and reputation that goes far beyond their operating number. The Stormtrooper who would earn the epithet “Ironarm” was among the first batch of enlisted men to join the Corps as clones began to fall out of favour.

He served with brutal, if undistinguished, efficiency until a mission pitted his squad against a rogue Jedi who had escaped the fall of the Order. He lost his squad and his arm in that fight, but took the Jedi down alone after her last reserves were spent. Legend among the Stormtrooper Corps has it that he earned his unofficial designation not because of his cybernetic replacement, but because he choked the life from his quarry with his remaining arm during the fight. Whatever the truth, Ironarm does not speak of it except through the pitiless gleam that emerges in his eyes at the mention of the Jedi.
gear: Stormtrooper armour and cybernetic prosthetics (+2 Soak)

abilities.name: Now This Is Podracing!

abilities.name: Force Power: Influence
abilities.description: The character may spend :forcepip: to stress the mind of one living target they are Engaged with, inflicting 1 strain. The Force user may make an opposed Discipline vs. Discipline check combined with an Influence power check. In the character spends :forcepip: and succeeds on the check, they can force the target to adopt an emotional state or believe something untrue, lasting for 3 rounds or 15 minutes.

abilities.name: Force Power: Move
abilities.description: The character can hurl objects by making a Move power check and rolling a ranged attack as part of the pool. The attack’s difficulty is equal to the silhouette of the object being thrown (default is silhouette 0) and only succeeds if they can also spend enough :forcepip: to move the object. The attack deals damage equal to the object’s silhouette times 10 (silhouette 0 deals 5 damage) plus 1 per net :success:. To throw multiple objects, they must generate enough :forcepip: to move multiple objects, and must use rules for Auto-Fire to determine difficulty and targeting.

abilities.description: The character can hurl objects by making a Move power check and rolling a ranged attack as part of the pool. The attack’s difficulty is equal to the silhouette of the object being thrown (default is silhouette 0) and only succeeds if they can also spend enough :forcepip: to move the object. The attack deals damage equal to the object’s silhouette times 10 (silhouette 0 deals 5 damage) plus 1 per net :success:. To throw multiple objects, they must generate enough :forcepip: to move multiple objects, and must use rules for Auto-Fire to determine difficulty and targeting.

abilities.name: Force Power: Sense
abilities.description: The character may spend :forcepip: to sense all living things within Short range (including animals and sentient beings) and may spend :forcepip::forcepip: to increase the range of this effect to Medium. abilities.name: Force Power: Sense
abilities.description: The character may spend :forcepip: to sense all living things within Short range (including animals and sentient beings) and may spend :forcepip::forcepip: to increase the range of this effect to Medium. The character may instead spend :forcepip: to sense the current emotional state of one living target they are engaged with.

name: Boba Fett (Child)

abilities.name: Jango’s Legacy
abilities.description: Once per session, Boba may upgrade his next combat check twice as an incidental.

abilities.name: Just A Kid
abilities.description: When making any social checks, Boba adds :setback::setback: unless the target has a reason to take him seriously.

gear: 2 Stun grenades, Boba’s light armour (+2 Soak)

abilities.name: Blaster Spiral
abilities.description: Once per encounter, Highsinger may suffer 4 strain to make a combat check with his light repeating blaster against the hardest to hit enemy (as determined by the GM) within Short range, with a base difficulty of :hard:. On success, every enemy in Short range suffers 1 hit that deals base damage plus additional :success:.

name: Cato Parasitti

abilities.name: Shape Shift
abilities.description: As an action, suffer 3 strain and make an :average: Resilience check to change appearance to a previously observed Silhouette 1 character. Observers must make an opposed Perception vs. Deception check to detect something is amiss.

gear: Light combat armour (+2 Soak), Holographic Disguise Matrix (add :boost::boost: to Deception checks to impersonate target; add :setback::setback: to onlookers’ Perception checks to detect imposter), Utility belt

name: Dengar (HoBS)

abilities.name: Brutal Hunter
abilities.description: If a target suffers Wounds from his attack, Dengar may suffer up to 3 Strain to inflict a number of additional Wounds equal to the amount of Strain suffered in this way.

abilities.name: Cantina Talk
abilities.description: When a character is within Short range of Dengar they may suffer 2 Strain to add automatic :advantage::advantage: to Streetwise and Knowledge: Underworld checks they make that turn.

abilities.name: Dirty Tricks
abilities.description: Once per encounter as an action, Dengar may make a :hard: Skulduggery check. If successful, he may select one enemy within Short range, plus one additional target per :success:, to suffer 4 Wounds. After resolving this, Dengar may perform a Move maneuver as an incidental.

gear: Modified heavy battle armour (+1 Defence, +2 Soak), Built-in comlink, Survival pack

abilities.name: Additional Limbs
abilities.description: Gain an additional free maneuver per turn, though may not perform more than 2 per turn.

abilities.name: Hoverer (Derrown)
abilities.description: Derrown may hover slightly off of the ground. When hovering, he does not have to spend additional maneuvers when navigating difficult terrain.

abilities.name: Electric Field
abilities.description: As an action, when engaged with an energy shield, Derrown may suffer 4 strain to make a :hard: Resilience check. On success, he may completely bypass the energy shield.

abilities.name: Improvised Transport
abilities.description: As a maneuver, Embo may convert his Kyuzo helmet into a Silhouette 1, Speed 2, Handling 0, System Strain Threshold 3 vehicle that can only operate on the ground and requires Piloting (Planetary) (:proficiency::proficiency::ability::ability:) to use. While using it in this way, Embo may not use the helmet’s weapon profiles and does not benefit from its Defensive or Deflection item qualities.

weapons.name: Kyuzo helmet (thrown)
weapons.notes: May spend :advantage::advantage::advantage: to recover helmet from ricochet.

abilities.name: Can’t Stand The Heat
abilities.description: Once per session Greedo may, as an action, roll a :daunting: Skulduggery or Streetwise check. If he succeeds, he immediately escapes the current encounter.

abilities.name: Distracting Quip
abilities.description: Once per encounter as an action, may make a :hard: Deception check. Each :success: gives Hondo +1 ranged and melee defence for the next two rounds.

abilities.name: Weequay Pheromones
abilities.description: Weequay may communicate with other Weequay using pheromones if they are within Short range of each other.

abilities.name: Delicate Information
abilities.description: Once per session, Ziro may spend a Destiny Point to reveal he possesses valuable blackmail material on any character in the encounter.

abilities.name: Ponderous
abilities.character: Ziro the Hutt, Geonosian Queen, Hydroid Medusa
abilities.description: Can never spend more than one maneuver moving per turn.

weapons.name: Smoke grenade
weapons.notes: Creates a thick black smoke that engulfs the target and anyone in Short range, adding :setback::setback::setback: to all Perception and Vigilance checks.

name: Moralo Eval

abilities.name: Criminal Mastermind
abilities.description: Eval uses Cunning for Negotiation and Underworld checks.

gear: 2 Frag grenades, Modified armour (+1 Defence, +2 Soak)

name: Pyke Leader

abilities.name: Ride The High
abilities.description: Once per session as an action, a Pyke Leader may use a single dose of Kessel Boost adrenaline to upgrade all combat checks once for the remainder of the encounter.

gear: Pyke armoured robes (+2 Soak), (4) Kessel Boost spice dose

name: Robonino
gear: Light utility armour (+2 Soak), Datapad, Slicer’s tools

abilities.name: Power Suit
abilities.description: Once per session, Seripas may spend :triumph: or :advantage::advantage::advantage: on a successful Ranged (Heavy) check to add the Breach 1 quality to the attack.

abilities.name: Knife Master
abilities.description: Whenever Sugi throws her vibroknife, it gains the Accurate 2 quality.

abilities.name: The Show Goes On
abilities.description: Whenever Sy Snootles is performing a song, allies gain :boost::boost::boost: on Stealth and Skulduggery checks against audience members.

name: Todo 360

abilities.name: Techno Service Droid
abilities.description: Once per session, Todo 360 may upgrade his next Computers check once as an incidental.

gear: Integrated slicer’s tools, Built-in encrypted comlink, Droid chassis (+1 Soak)

abilities.name: Force Power: Bind
abilities.description: Dooku may spend :forcepip: to immobilise a target within Short range until the end of his next turn. If Dooku used :darkside: to generate :forcepip:, the target also suffers 1 wound per :forcepip: spent on the check, ignoring soak. May spend :forcepip::forcepip: to add up to two additional targets.

abilities.name: Force Power: Enhance
abilities.description: When making an Athletics, Coordination or Resilience check, the character may roll an Enhance power check as part of the pool and may spend :forcepip: to gain :success: or :advantage: (their choice) on the check. May make an Enhance power check as a maneuver to spend :forcepip: to leap to any location within Short range, or :forcepip::forcepip: to leap to any location within Medium range.

abilities.name: Force Power: Move
abilities.description: The character can hurl objects by making a Move power check and rolling a ranged attack as part of the pool. The attack’s difficulty is equal to the silhouette of the object being thrown (default is silhouette 0) and only succeeds if they can also spend enough :forcepip: to move the object. The attack deals damage equal to the object’s silhouette times 10 (silhouette 0 deals 5 damage) plus 1 per net :success:. To throw multiple objects, they must generate enough :forcepip: to move multiple objects, and must use rules for Auto-Fire to determine difficulty and targeting.

abilities.name: Force Power: Protect
abilities.description: Dooku may make an :average: Discipline check as part of a Protect power check. May spend :forcepip::forcepip: to reduce damage from an energy weapon that hits Dooku by 4 plus 1 per net :success:.

abilities.name: Force Power: Sense
abilities.description: The character may spend :forcepip: to sense all living things within Short range (including animals and sentient beings) and may spend :forcepip::forcepip: to increase the range of this effect to Medium. The character may instead spend :forcepip: to sense the current emotional state of one living target they are engaged with.

abilities.name: Force Power: Unleash
abilities.description: Dooku may make an :average: Discipline check as part of an Unleash power check. May spend :forcepip::forcepip: to make an attack dealing 4 damage plus 1 per net :success:, with a critical rating of 4, to a target at Short range. May spend :forcepip: to add 3 damage to the attack, or :advantage: to inflict 1 strain on the target.

weapons.name: Dooku’s Lightsaber
weapons.notes: Add :darkside: to Force power checks.

abilities.name: Infiltrate And Execute
abilities.description: May impersonate a known enemy’s voice to gain :boost::boost: on all Deception checks, but upgrades the difficulty once if also attempting to maintain a physical disguise (such as wearing an enemy uniform).

abilities.name: Disguise Mode
abilities.description: As a maneuver, may transform into the appearance of an innocent sweeper droid. If no enemies witness the transformation, the droid immediately gains Deception 3 (:proficiency::proficiency::ability:) as a skill.

abilities.name: Self Destruct
abilities.description: As a maneuver, may transform into a ticking bomb. In this mode, the droid may not move, communicate or use its integrated blasters. As an action, it may detonate using the Self Destruct weapon profile. This instantly destroys the droid.

abilities.name: Lancer
abilities.description: When piloting a swoop and crossing from Long to Engaged range in a single turn to attack a target, the IG combat lance gains the Breach 1 quality for that turn.

abilities.name: Lumbering
abilities.description: May only make one Move maneuver per turn and may not take the Aim maneuver.

abilities.name: Regeneration (Durge)
abilities.description: When recovering from wounds via natural rest or in a bacta tank, recover 1 additional Wound. First-aid, medical treatment, and stimpacks do not result in recovery from an additional wound. Durge can regrow lost limbs.

abilities.name: Undying
abilities.description: At the end of each round, Durge makes a :daunting: Resilience check to recover wounds equal to :success:.

abilities.name: Rocket Pack
abilities.description: Allows Teska to function as a Silhouette 1, Speed 2, Handling 0, System Strain Threshold 6 vehicle that requires Piloting (Planetary) (:proficiency::proficiency::proficiency:) to operate.

name: Lux Bonteri
gear: Upper class clothing (+1 Soak), or padded armour (+2 Soak)

abilities.name: Diplomatic Solution
abilities.description: Once per session, at the GM’s discretion, when a combat encounter against one or more sentient creatures is about to begin, Mina Bonteri may spend a Destiny Point and make a :daunting: Charm check to turn the encounter into a social encounter instead.

abilities.name: Getting Results
abilities.description: When accompanied by armed guards, Gunray gains Coercion 2 (:proficiency::proficiency::ability:) for as long as they are within Short range.

abilities.name: Neimoidian Courage
abilities.description: When all allied NPCs protecting this Neimoidian have been defeated, they must make a :hard: Discipline check. If they fail, they surrender unconditionally.

abilities.name: Shoot Them Or Something!
abilities.description: Once per encounter, Gunray may nominate an enemy combatant as a primary target. All allies gain :boost: on attacks made against the target.

abilities.name: Clever But Crazy
abilities.description: Dr Vindi may suffer 3 strain to make a skill check using Intellect instead of the characteristic associated with the skill.

weapons.name: Bio-virus grenade
weapons.notes: All targets suffering at least 1 Wound, or are within Medium range without environmental protection, must make a :formidable: Resilience check. Target suffers 2 Wounds and 2 Strain per :threat: and a Vicious 5 Critical Injury if the check is failed. :despair: means they must make the check again at the the start of the next turn.

abilities.name: Hound Master
abilities.description: May spend a maneuver to direct any trained beast within Medium range. The beast may perform an immediate free maneuver or add :boost: to their next skill check.

abilities.name: Temper Tantrum
abilities.description: When Sobeck fails a combat check he suffers 1 strain but gains 1 automatic :success: on his next attack against the same target, so long as he attacks them on his next consecutive turn.

abilities.name: Flyer
abilities.description: This creature can fly.

abilities.name: Geonosian Valour
abilities.description: May perform a maneuver to cause all ranged attacks targeting the character to instead hit one ally or helpless enemy in Engaged range, until the beginning of the character’s next turn.

name: Riff Tamson
gear: 4 Karkarodon grenades, Aquatic assault suit (+1 Defence, +2 Soak)

abilities.name: Duplicitous
abilities.description: Once per session as an action, Clovis may make a :hard: Charm or Deception check. On success, he cannot be a target of combat checks until the end of the encounter or until making a combat check himself.

name: Separatist Commander

abilities.name: Concentrate Your Fire!
abilities.description: May perform a maneuver to direct one allied minion group within Medium range; the group may perform an immediate free combat check action.

gear: Armoured uniform (+1 Defence, +2 Soak), Encrypted comlink

abilities.name: Blademaster
abilities.description: Sora Bulq does not increase difficulty when making attacks with two weapons.

abilities.name: Force Power: Bind
abilities.description: Bulq may spend :forcepip: to immobilise a target within Short range until the end of his next turn. If Bulq used :darkside: to generate :forcepip:, the target also suffers 1 wound per :forcepip: spent on the check, ignoring soak.

abilities.name: Force Power: Enhance
abilities.description: When making an Athletics, Coordination or Resilience check, the character may roll an Enhance power check as part of the pool and may spend :forcepip: to gain :success: or :advantage: (their choice) on the check. May make an Enhance power check as a maneuver to spend :forcepip: to leap to any location within Short range, or :forcepip::forcepip: to leap to any location within Medium range.

abilities.name: Force Power: Move
abilities.description: The character can hurl objects by making a Move power check and rolling a ranged attack as part of the pool. The attack’s difficulty is equal to the silhouette of the object being thrown (default is silhouette 0) and only succeeds if they can also spend enough :forcepip: to move the object. The attack deals damage equal to the object’s silhouette times 10 (silhouette 0 deals 5 damage) plus 1 per net :success:. To throw multiple objects, they must generate enough :forcepip: to move multiple objects, and must use rules for Auto-Fire to determine difficulty and targeting.

abilities.name: Force Power: Sense
abilities.description: The character may spend :forcepip: to sense all living things within Short range (including animals and sentient beings) and may spend :forcepip::forcepip: to increase the range of this effect to Medium. The character may instead spend :forcepip: to sense the current emotional state of one living target they are engaged with.

name: Wat Tambor

abilities.name: Methane Breather
abilities.description: Requires methane to live and must wear a special pressure suit. Treats standard planetary air pressure and composition as a corrosive atmosphere with rating 6.

abilities.name: Sudden Leverage
abilities.description: Once per session, Wat Tambor may spend a Destiny Point to produce a piece of highly incriminating information about another character in the encounter. This information can be genuine, or a very high quality counterfeit, at the GM’s discretion.

gear: Skakoan pressure suit (+2 Soak), Datapad, Encrypted comlink

name: BoSS Agent
description: The Bureau of Ships and Services is one of the most pervasive facets of the Imperial bureaucracy, mostly because it existed long before the formation of the Galactic Empire. The earliest incarnation of the BoSS was established when hyperspace travel first became prevalent in the galaxy, thousands of years before the advent of the Galactic Empire. The primary responsibility of the organization is to assign, regulate, and track the use of transponder codes, which are unique to each individual space-going vessel. Any ships identified as participating in any form of illegal activity, such as involvement with the Rebellion, are flagged in the massive BoSS database and can be identified in almost any major starport throughout the galaxy. For this reason, using false transponder codes is common practice in criminal circles as well as by the Alliance. BoSS agents who supervise such oddities are therefore a significant threat to criminals and Rebels alike.
gear: Secure datapad with uplink to BoSS database

name: COMPNOR Agent
description: The Committee for the Preservation of the New Order is a name used to describe multiple branches of the Imperial bureaucracy, all of which are completely devoted to maintaining support for the Emperor’s rule. The precursor agency, COMPOR, was established as a grassroots political movement during the Clone Wars era, and was a powerful tool for maintaining support for the Supreme Chancellor. After the establishment of the Empire, the organization was reorganized to support the Empire as a whole rather than the Emperor specifically, although he factors significantly in their extensive, pervasive propaganda campaigns. COMPNOR agents are easily the most overzealous of all Imperial agents and genuinely believe that any and all horrors committed in the execution of their duty to root out disloyalty are for the greater good of the galaxy.

name: Government Bureaucrat
description: The machinery of the Galactic Empire runs on bureaucracy, and legions of bureaucrats keep those wheels turning at all times. Generally dour, punctual, and boring, many of these individuals manage to accomplish the near-impossible task of being more reviled than Imperial Stormtroopers.

name: Imperial Advisor
description: One of the most insidious methods the Emperor employs to maintain control of his Empire is constantly pitting his minions against one another. These mind games allow such individuals to gain favor by proving their ultimate loyalty to him. Historically, this has prevented Palpatine’s subordinates from allying against him, and nowhere has this policy been more ruthlessly embodied than in the ranks of his Imperial advisors. Typically, there have been around one hundred such extremely powerful and influential bureaucrats at any given time. Each is tasked not only with overseeing details of the Empire’s administration, including such important decisions as appointing governors and Moffs, and also with conducting covert surveillance on the home systems of other advisors and high-ranking Imperial officials.

name: Imperial DeStab Agent
description: The Destabilization Branch is a particular agency within Imperial Intelligence, and is regarded by the Alliance as the most evil of all Imperial facets. The purpose of Destabilization is to ensure that sufficient fear and terror grips the galactic populace such that they rely upon the Galactic Empire to maintain social order and convention. DeStab agents tend to be exceptionally patriotic, but are often sociopathic in the execution of their duties, and have no regard for life whatsoever, not even among the ranks of other Imperials. Some of the most significant atrocities of the Galactic Civil War were instigated by DeStab agents, although some were avoided thanks to Rebel Alliance intervention. Relatively early during the conflict, surgically modified DeStab agents infiltrated the ranks of the Mon Calamari and Quarren races on the planet Mon Calamari and attempted to spark a war between the two, to ensure that they would be unable to supply the Rebel Alliance with the starships needed to maintain its struggle against the Imperial Navy. The Alliance thwarted the attempt, but sadly, many similar plots throughout the galaxy have gone undetected until it was far too late.

name: Imperial Intelligence Agent
description: The resources at the disposal of the Imperial Intelligence Bureau are virtually without limit and the agents who enact the Bureau’s will throughout the galaxy are highly trained, extremely motivated, and absolutely lethal. Although their mandates and methodologies vary from agent to agent, the essential truth of the matter is that an intelligence agent will do absolutely anything to ensure that whatever problem he or she has been dispatched to deal with is, in fact, dealt with permanently. If an agent determines that an individual may pose a threat to the stability of the Empire, either in the present or in the future, he may well forsake traditional measures and take it upon himself to ensure that the htreat is neutralised.

name: Imperial Moff
description: The term “moff” predates the establishment of the Galactic Republic, much less the Galactic Empire. Never has its use been more prevalent than since the Emperor co-opted it for his own use and created the position of Imperial Moff for individuals assigned to oversee an entire sector of space in the Empire’s name. Those chosen to serve as Moffs are either extremely loyal, extremely capable, or easily manipulated. Regardless, having one Imperial representative to whom other agents, such as planetary governors or system overseers, report minimizes the necessary number of personnel at the top of the hierarchy. Each Moff reports either to an Imperial advisor or, after the establishment of the Tarkin Doctrine, to a Grand Moff responsible for the governance of an oversector. Individual Moffs have incredible leeway in the organization and dispensation of resources under their command, up to and including the military assets assigned to a particular sector.

name: Detention Centre Staff
gear: Detention centre uniform or maintenance coverall

name: Imperial Army Trooper
description: The common citizen of the galaxy will never see a Star Destroyer or a stormtrooper in person, if they are fortunate. For the majority of those who never leave a planet’s surface, the Imperial Army trooper is the face of the Empire. Stormtroopers and starships can be seen in promotional materials, but it is the stern and unforgiving face of the Army infantry that enforces the will of the Emperor on a planet’s surface. For that reason, it is the men and women of the Imperial Army whom Alliance personnel must combat.

name: Imperial Assassin
description: The shadowy corps of Imperial assassins exercises the Emperor’s will by eliminating any sentient its master wishes. No matter the target, an Imperial assassin tirelessly pursues him across the galaxy until he can get close enough for a kill. In their travels throughout the galaxy, Imperial assassins are hidden in plain sight, often taking on disguies to grant them easier access to their prey. The waiter at the tapcafe, the harried merchant across the counter, even the carefully vetted bodyguard–any of these may be assassins waiting to strike.

name: Imperial Dungeoneer
description: Imperial dungeoneers are soldiers tasked with overssing the prisoners confined to the Empire’s many dungeon ships, detention facilities, and prison worlds. Despite the sensitive nature of their task, those chosen as guards for the Empire’s enormous population of incarcerated sentients are usually those who couldn’t make the grade in the regular Imperial Army. Considered lowly turnkeys by their compatriots, dungeoneers tend to be bitter and cruel, taking their resentment over thier miserable existence out on their helpless charges.

name: Imperial Gunnery Corps
description: The Gunnery Corps is a subdivision of the Imperial military that includes members of both the Imperial Army and the Imperial Navy—although there are more of the latter than the former. As with troops in the Vehicle Corps, with whom they often work, members of the Gunnery Corps receive rudimentary training as troopers, but the bulk of their instruction is in the use of vehicle- or installation-mounted weaponry. The ubiquitous turbolasers that are found on the hundreds of different starships in use in the Imperial Navy, as well as the vast variety of lasers that are deployed in ground installations and the many vehicles employed by the Vehicle Corps, are all fired by members of the Gunnery Corps.

name: Imperial Army Officer
description: An incredible number of officers are required by the Imperial military simply to oversee day-to-day operations and orchestrate major offensives. The number of officers presently serving has increased markedly in the past few years, owed to the growing threat of the Rebel Alliance. As has been the case with literally countless militaries throughout galactic history, merit is the surest means of rising through the ranks of the Imperial military, but there is also a very strong political component. Many officers have little more to their name than their family connections.

name: Imperial Navy Trooper
description: Often confused with the rank-and-file of the Imperial Army, much to the consternation of both groups, Naval troopers fulfill essentially the same purpose for the Imperial Navy. They are the enforcement arm that can be found on large-scale starships and naval space stations. Standard duties for naval troopers include the supervision of any and all prisoners aboard a vessel, simple guard duty, escort details for important guests, and boarding actions against enemy vessels.

name: M226 Navy Trooper
description: The Navy troopers assigned to the Perlemian Haul’s freighters are pulled from some of the Imperial Navy’s best personnel. As such, they are highly motivated and willing to die for the Empire if need be. A group of four of these troopers, commanded by Petty Ofhcer Wen, is assigned to provide security in M226’s loading bay.

name: Imperial Navy Officer
description: Officers in the Imperial Navy are highly trained, often at a military academy. Most of their duties naturally fall into the category of ship-bound activities, monitoring the hundreds or thousands of personnel necessary to maintain the massive capital-scale ships the navy depends upon However, it is also possible that officers are deployed at the head of naval troopers, taking part in ship-to-ship actions against pirates or independent vessels or. even in sorties on the ground. In these cases, most naval officers prefer to lead from the rear, using their superior training and experience to direct the activities of their subordinates. Despite their reluctance to “get their hands dirty,” it is unwise to underestimate naval officers.

name: Imperial Royal Guard
description: Numbering among the deadliets of the Emperor’s servants, his elite, crimson-garbed Imperial Royal Guards enjoy a reputation for preternatural combat skills and absolute unwavering loyalty to their master. Only the most skilled and dependable fighters are chosen from the many branches of Imperial service to undergo Royal Guard training, and only a few survive this training to be unducted into the ranks of these elite warriors.

name: Imperial Security Detail
notes: Wound Threshold has been reduced to 5 as the 10 in the adventure seems wrong for a minion squad.

name: Imperial Test Pilot
description: The pilots of the 481 st Experimental Starfighter Test ing Squadron are the best of the best, hand picked by Gareth Agamar for their piloting skills, courage, and technical acumen. Charged with testing out new and potentially dangerous fightercraft, these individuals are looked on by rank-and-file Navy pilots with equal parts admiration and pity: admiration due to their obvious skill and the prestigious nature of their roles as test pilots, but pity due to the fact that flying an untested spaceframe with new and perilous engines and weapon systems is extremely dangerous. Turnover in the 481st is particularly high for what is ostensibly a non-combat unit, and new pilots are constantly being brought into the squadron as old pilots are injured or killed.
gear: Sensory augmentation package (add :advantage: to all Perception checks)

name: Imperial Vehicle Corps
description: A subsidiary branch of the Imperial Army, the Vehicle Corps is a specialized division of troopers who receive rudimentary infantry training but specialize in piloting the various forms of vehicles utilized by that military organization. This includes not only land and sea vehicles, but aircraft that cannot achieve orbit on their own (any orbital craft falls under the auspices of the Imperial Navy). Repulsorlift operations, ground vehicle operations, and aquatic vehicle operations all fall under this umbrella; however, the lattermost of these is typically grouped in with the subdivision of the Imperial Army known as the lesser Imperial Navy, responsible for waterborne operations on the surfaces of planets throughout the galaxy.

name: Intelligence Agent
description: Imperial Intelligence is a separate entity from the military, but they often work hand-in-hand to accomplish their mutual objectives. The autonomy of Imperial Intelligence often allows them to co-opt military assets to accomplish their missions, occasionally to the chagrin of the officers who command the ships or companies utilized in such a manner. Regardless, the threat posed by intelligence agents is particularly significant, because they do not adhere to standard military protocol and are easily able to think outside the rigid tactical instruction of the Army and Navy. Seasoned agents understand how the Alliance thinks, and that makes them exceptionally dangerous.

name: Military Informant
description: These individuals (or, as the Empire refers to them, intelligence assets) are a pale reflection of the agents from the Imperial Intelligence Bureau. However, they are equally loyal and enjoy the benehts of deep, virtually impenetrable cover. Suitable candidates are selected at a very early age and discreetly indoctrinated when there is a reasonable explanation for their absence. These individuals are instilled with great loyalty to the Imperial military complex in exchange for extremely meager rewards in the grand scheme of things, whether they are military, monetary, or political in nature. These men and women are given legitimate civilian reasons to be moved t o political hotspots, where they carefully monitor the population for any signs of treason or paramilitary activity. They then dutifully report this information using blind drops, encrypted communiques, or other secretive means.

name: Naval Engineer
description: The Imperial Corps of Engineers is technically a separate branch of the military, but is most often associated with the Imperial Navy due to the frequent presence of its representatives aboard the Navy’s starships. Their primary purpose within the Navy is to serve as tactical advisors for the frequent sieges that Star Destroyers are called upon to implement. They can also be found among the ranks of the Imperial Army, where their purpose is more constructive, facilitating the creation of military installations for use in ground operations.

name: M226 Bridge Crew
description: The bridge crew of M226 are an experienced lot, and many of them have served aboard the heavy freighter since it was first commissioned. Though loyal to the Empire and willing to follow Noor’s orders, they are much more comfortable under the command of their executive officer, Lieutenant Troo.

name: M226 Crew Chief
description: The typical naval crew chief is a seasoned veteran who has been serving aboard Imperial ships for many years. Crew chiefs are the most technically proficient in their areas of expertise and are often looked up to by their subordinates. Like other crew, they wear grey Navy jumpsuits, but their rank is easily distinguished by the insignia on their lapel.

name: M226 Navy Crewman
description: The crew of M226 are typical Navy personnel with very little to set them apart from their peers on other ships. They are hard-working and loyal, but aren’t extraordinary in any way. They tend to work in groups of 3 or 4 individuals. Each one wears a grey Navy jumpsuit.

name: Imperial Stormtrooper
description:
There is no greater symbol of the Emperor’s New Order than the elite stormtrooper legions that enforce his will without remorse, mercy, or compassion. The featureless helmets, the uniformity of their armour, and the sheer number of them advancing without care for the losses they sustain, all contribute to their intractable image. The details of the Clone Wars have been long forgotten by the majority of the galaxy’s denizens. As a result, there are precious few who associate the terrifying modern stormtroopers with the once-heroic clone troopers who waged that conflict on behalf of the Galactic Republic.

The Imperial Stormtrooper Corps is an independent branch of the military, and while they answer to the officers of both the Army and the Navy, their own commanders have the discretion to ignore or overrule orders issued to them if they feel they are not in the best interests of the Galactic Empire. There are numerous specialities within the stormtrooper legions, the majority of which use equipment that meshes with their training to create a cadre of elite troops that can effectively serve in virtually any combat theatre.
notes: Individuals or groups of two may be armed with light repeating blasters with slings.

name: Stormtrooper Sergeant
description: Stormtroopers who survive engagements with high marks are promoted to the position of sergeant. Typically this involves command of a single squadron, but stormtroopers who excel at leadership are promoted to higher ranks. Such positions can include supervision of entire companies of stormtroopers. Rank within the stormtrooper legions is difficult for outsiders to fully understand, as they refer to virtually any officer from their ranks as commander. The uniformity of their armour and this tendency for vague references helps to enforce the image of stormtroopers as an absolutely faceless, monolithic military entity, which only serves to enhance their fearsome reputation.

name: Stormtrooper: Dark Trooper
description: Funded and operated as a subdivision of the Imperial Stormtrooper Corps, the Dark Trooper Project was a clandestine attempt to create an even more fearsome elite fighting force which was easier to produce within a short period of time. The goal of the project was to create extremely advanced military droids that could pass for armoured humans, thus achieving the fearsome aspect required of elite military units without any concern for the environmental conditions that might limit human personnel. Although sabotaged by Alliance efforts, a small number of prototypes were completed, and the Rebellion lives in perpetual fear that the project may be continued in another, as yet unknown, area.

name: Stormtrooper: Sandtrooper
description: Desert worlds are often rich in minerals or other infrequently appearing resources, necessitating settlement despite the harshness of their environments. The extreme temperatures, as well as the abrasiveness and navigational problems associated with the terrain, necessitate specialized training and equipment. Sandtroopers are intimately familiar with arid environments, and are trained in beast-riding as a means of enduring transportation under conditions that might otherwise cripple conventional forms of technology.
notes: Individuals or groups of two may also have riding dewbacks.
gear: High-temp stormtrooper armour (+2 Soak, remove :setback::setback: imposed due to arid or hot environmental conditions)

name: Stormtrooper: Scout Trooper
description: Reconnaissance is one of the most important aspects of any military organization, and the Imperial Stormtrooper Corps is no different. More lightly armoured to facilitate rapid movement and trained with rapid-response repulsorlift vehicles, scout troopers are at the forefront of any insertion of the stormtrooper legions into a new theater of operations.

name: Stormtrooper: Swamptrooper
